base_read._comment: Base table for the HDL load of this object is the balance batch line, PAY_BAL_BATCH_LINES, joined to its header PAY_BAL_BATCH_HEADERS on this run's prefixed batch name DMTW2<prefix>. NOTE: use the PAY_BAL_BATCH_* tables (the live, current tables), NOT PAY_BALANCE_BATCH_* -- that similarly-named replica is stale by months in this BIP relay (max creation_date frozen at 2026-04-14). HDL upload+load writes the accepted lines here (the good rows), keyed by PAYROLL_RELATIONSHIP_NUMBER. The bad line references a nonexistent parent batch name (DMTW2<prefix>NOSUCH), which HDL cannot resolve, so it errors at load and no line is written -- proven by the load-time HDL message plus absence from this base read. v2 seeded: the LDG name, the two good payroll relationship numbers (2, 2852), the bad line's relationship (2854), and the two payroll names (Biweekly, Retail Biweekly) are hard-coded standard seeded demo values -- confirmed present on the pod via read-only BIP, never records we loaded. The batch name keeps ${PREFIX} so each run is a brand-new batch and is naturally re-runnable. The downstream Load Initial Balances ESS process then converts the accepted batch lines (status U = unprocessed) into actual balances in PAY_RUN_BALANCES -- that step is documented in GOLD_README but is not part of the standalone HDL load path.
base_read.sql: SELECT l.payroll_relationship_number AS PRN, MAX(l.batch_line_id) AS BATCH_LINE_ID, MAX(l.batch_line_status) AS BATCH_LINE_STATUS, MAX(l.balance_name) AS BALANCE_NAME, MAX(l.value) AS VAL FROM pay_bal_batch_lines l JOIN pay_bal_batch_headers h ON h.batch_id=l.batch_id WHERE h.batch_name='DMTW2${PREFIX}' AND h.legislative_data_group_id=300000046974970 GROUP BY l.payroll_relationship_number
